content:
First note that if $\deg f=d$ then in the identity

 f(x−1)+f(x)+f(x+1)=f(x)^2/(2013x)

the left–hand side has degree $d$, while the right–hand side has degree $2d−1$.  Equating,
$$d=2d-1\quad\Longrightarrow\quad d=1.$$
Hence write
$$f(x)=Ax+B,\;A\ne0.$$
Then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)
=(A(x-1)+B)+(Ax+B)+(A(x+1)+B)
=3Ax+3B,
\]
while
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}.
\]
Multiplying both sides by $2013x$ gives the polynomial identity
\[
2013x\,(3Ax+3B)\;=\;A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2.
\]
Comparing coefficients:

• $x^2$: $2013\cdot3A=6039A=A^2\;\Longrightarrow\;A=0\text{ or }6039.$  
Since $A\ne0$, $A=6039$.

• $x^1$: $2013\cdot3B=6039B=2AB=2\cdot6039B\;\Longrightarrow\;B=0.$

• $\,$constant: $0=B^2$ (okay).

Thus the unique non‐constant solution is
$$f(x)=6039\,x,$$
and hence
$$f(1)=6039.$$
Answer: \(\boxed{6039}.\)

Hence write

Then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)
=(A(x-1)+B)+(Ax+B)+(A(x+1)+B)
=3Ax+3B,
\]
while
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}.
\]
Multiplying both sides by $2013x$ gives the polynomial identity
\[
2013x\,(3Ax+3B)\;=\;A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2.

$$f(x)=6039\,x,$$

Answer: \(\boxed{6039}.\)
